app: Quenderin native store apps (iOS SwiftUI + QuenderinKit; Android Kotlin/Compose ai.quenderin.app)
scope: Whether the native iOS + Android store apps can pass App Store + Google Play review. Offline on-device LLM: onboarding -> model picker -> user-initiated GGUF download from HuggingFace -> llama.cpp on-device inference -> chat + pure-logic agent (calculator/unit-convert/date). No account, backend, analytics. ADB device control is a SEPARATE desktop Electron app, not in scope.

verdict.summary: Neither store can be submitted today. iOS and Android each have exactly 2 hard blockers. All blockers are submission-mechanics or one Android manifest line with cheap, well-scoped fixes; the underlying app design (offline, no data collection, minimal permissions, no device automation, targetSdk current) is genuinely review-friendly.
iosBlockers: PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy absent -> App Store Connect upload validator auto-rejects the binary
No privacy-policy URL -> App Store Connect record cannot be submitted for review (hard field)
androidBlockers: No <service> declaring foregroundServiceType=dataSync -> MissingForegroundServiceTypeException crash on Android 14+ during the model download
No privacy-policy URL -> Play Console listing / Data Safety form cannot be completed

findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 5.1.1; Apple Privacy Manifest policy (mandatory for all new submissions/updates since 2024-05-01)
findings.evidence: find over apple/ returns zero PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy (re-confirmed this session). Required-reason APIs used: disk-space volumeAvailableCapacityForImportantUsageKey + attributesOfFileSystem (DiskSpace.swift:24-28); file-size attributesOfItem .size (OfflineReadiness.swift:52-53, Preflight.swift:66); writes to .applicationSupportDirectory (OnboardingModel.swift:127). Upload validator rejects binaries missing the manifest or using required-reason APIs without a declared reason. llama.xcframework slices also lack a manifest.
findings.remediation: Add apple/QuenderinKit/Sources/QuenderinKit/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy (or app target): NSPrivacyTracking=false, NSPrivacyTrackingDomains=[], NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypes=[], NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypes with disk-space reason E174.1 and file-attribute reason 3B52.1. Confirm llama.xcframework manifest coverage.

findings.item: No privacy-policy URL (also a Play blocker)
findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 5.1.1; App Store Connect requires a privacy-policy URL for every app
findings.evidence: docs/STORE_SUBMISSION.md:13-25 ('Both stores reject without a privacy policy URL'). No URL in any Swift source. RootView/ChatView/AgentView have no Settings/About/Privacy row.
findings.remediation: Host a short static policy (GitHub Pages per STORE_SUBMISSION.md:24). Fill the URL in App Store Connect (hard gating field). Add an in-app About/Privacy row.

findings.item: UIBackgroundModes not declared while BackgroundModelDownloader uses a background URLSession
findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 2.5.4; Apple QA1941
findings.evidence: BackgroundModelDownloader.swift:31 creates URLSessionConfiguration.background(withIdentifier:) with sessionSendsLaunchEvents=true. Neither Info.plist (1-30) nor project.yml (1-27) declare UIBackgroundModes. Shipping path QuenderinApp.swift:21 wires foreground URLSessionModelDownloader, so latent; but the class is in the bundled framework (visible to static analysis) and handleEventsForBackgroundURLSession is absent.
findings.remediation: If background download is intended: add UIBackgroundModes (fetch or BGProcessingTaskRequest) and implement application(_:handleEventsForBackgroundURLSession:completionHandler:)/.backgroundTask. If foreground is the shipping path: mark BackgroundModelDownloader as non-shipping.

findings.item: No EULA / Terms - Apple Standard EULA not opted in
findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 5.1.1; App Store Connect -> App Information -> EULA
findings.evidence: STORE_SUBMISSION.md:17 covers only the privacy gap. Submit for Review stays greyed out until a custom EULA URL or Apple's Standard EULA is selected.
findings.remediation: Select 'Use Apple's Standard End User License Agreement' in App Information. One click, no code.

findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 2.5.4/2.5.8; US EAR 742.15(b) exemption
findings.evidence: Info.plist (1-30) has no key. App uses only OS TLS + CryptoKit SHA-256 (exempt). STORE_SUBMISSION.md:44 documents the fix; not done. Triggers the export questionnaire on every upload (Missing Compliance state).
findings.remediation: Add <key>ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption</key><false/> to Info.plist (or project.yml properties). One line.

findings.item: Guideline 4.2 minimum functionality - app is a near-empty shell until a 0.4-9 GB download completes
findings.evidence: RootView.swift:20-35 gates all post-onboarding UI behind 'if case .ready = onboarding.phase'. 9 GB model can be 30-60 min on Wi-Fi; no demo/cached mode before ready.
findings.remediation: Risk low - onboarding (OnboardingModel.swift phases probing->recommended->downloading->loading->ready) is purposeful, not blank. Provide complete App Review notes (size range, be on Wi-Fi, onboarding IS first-use by design) per STORE_SUBMISSION.md:47-49. Optional read-only demo mode.

findings.item: Foreground service type not declared - no <service> element in AndroidManifest.xml
findings.policyRef: Google Play Foreground Service Policy; Android 14 (API 34) FGS-type enforcement; targetSdk 35 makes it mandatory
findings.evidence: AndroidManifest.xml has zero <service> elements (re-confirmed this session; manifest ends after the single <activity>). ModelDownloadWorker.kt:65-71 passes FOREGROUND_SERVICE_TYPE_DATA_SYNC at runtime; FOREGROUND_SERVICE_DATA_SYNC declared at manifest:9. WorkManager 2.9.1 does not auto-inject android:foregroundServiceType. On API 34+, setForeground() with an undeclared type throws MissingForegroundServiceTypeException - crashing during the model download (primary setup step).
findings.remediation: Add <service android:name="androidx.work.impl.foreground.SystemForegroundService" android:foregroundServiceType="dataSync" tools:node="merge" /> and xmlns:tools to <manifest>. Declare DATA_SYNC in Play Console -> App Content -> foreground service types.

findings.item: No privacy-policy URL (also an iOS blocker)
findings.policyRef: Google Play User Data policy (privacy policy required in Play Console field AND in-app for all apps)
findings.evidence: STORE_SUBMISSION.md section 0: no hosted/in-app policy. No 'privacy' reference in OnboardingScreen.kt, ChatScreen.kt, AgentScreen.kt, MainTabs.kt. Play Console submission cannot complete without it.
findings.remediation: Host the same short static policy; link in the Play Console listing field and an in-app About/Privacy row.

findings.item: No user mechanism to flag/report AI-generated content
findings.policyRef: Google Play Generative AI Policy (effective Dec 2023) - applies to on-device and cloud alike
findings.evidence: ChatScreen.kt (114 lines) renders MessageBubble (58-59) with no long-press/overflow/flag. AgentScreen.kt (121 lines) renders AgentStepRow (69) + final-answer Surface (70-76) with no report action.
findings.remediation: Add a long-press/overflow 'Report this response' on AI bubbles (ChatScreen) and agent answers (AgentScreen). A mailto: or in-app form to a developer address satisfies the policy; no moderation pipeline required.

findings.item: SafetyBlocklist gates agent tool inputs but not chat output
findings.policyRef: Google Play Generative AI Policy ('implement safeguards to minimize the risk of outputting policy-violating content'); Inappropriate Content policy
findings.evidence: AgentLoop.kt:49-54 applies SafetyBlocklist.isBlocked() to decision.input/decision.name only. ChatScreen.kt:76-93 -> chat.send(text) returns model output unfiltered. SafetyBlocklist.kt:9-17 covers financial/destructive/credential only - no hate/violence/CSAM. Unconstrained GGUF can emit policy-violating chat text.
findings.remediation: Add an output-side keyword/regex check in ChatModel/inference result path (on-device, no cloud call). Expand SafetyBlocklist categories for chat and agent paths. 'Minimize risk' language -> keyword filter suffices.

findings.policyRef: Google Play Data Safety form accuracy; Android backup best practice
findings.evidence: AndroidManifest.xml:14 allowBackup=true, no dataExtractionRules/fullBackupContent. ModelDownloadWorker.kt:51 stores 0.4-9 GB GGUF at filesDir/models. Drive backup silently skips (25 MB cap); ADB backup would attempt multi-GB archives.
findings.remediation: Add res/xml/backup_rules.xml with <exclude domain="file" path="models/" />; reference via android:dataExtractionRules (API 31+) and android:fullBackupContent (API <=30). Reflect conversation-history backup stance in the Data Safety form.

findings.item: Data Safety form: HuggingFace network access must be disclosed accurately
findings.policyRef: Google Play Data Safety form policy
findings.evidence: WorkManagerModelDownloader.kt enqueues a GET to HuggingFace (URL from ModelCatalog). Carries no user data, but INTERNET permission visible in the manifest could prompt a reviewer mismatch with 'no data shared.'
findings.remediation: Answer 'No data collected'/'No data shared' (accurate - GET of a public file). Add a store-description note: 'The only network activity is downloading your chosen model from HuggingFace on first setup. No user data ever leaves your device.'

findings.item: Privacy-policy URL missing - both stores require one before review
findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 5.1.1; Google Play User Data policy
findings.evidence: STORE_SUBMISSION.md:16-17. No privacy_policy_url in any store-metadata file; none in Info.plist/AndroidManifest.xml.
findings.remediation: Host one short static page; link in App Store Connect, Play Console, and an in-app About/Privacy row. (Tracked per-store as IOS-B2 / AND-B2; this is the cross-cutting framing - counted once.)

findings.item: LLM/chat output entirely unmoderated - blocklist gates only agent tool calls
findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 1.2 (objectionable AI content); Google Play AI-Generated Content policy
findings.evidence: SafetyBlocklist.swift:1-3 docstring scopes it to agent tool actions. AgentLoop.swift:63-68 runs the check inside the .useTool branch only; .finalAnswer (59-61) and direct chat completions return verbatim, unfiltered.
findings.remediation: (1) One-time onboarding disclaimer; (2) a Report/Flag affordance (mailto: satisfies the checklist); (3) optional lightweight output-side keyword check showing a warning banner.

findings.item: Age rating must be filed 17+ (iOS) / Mature 17+ (Android)
findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 1.2 + Age Rating questionnaire (Unrestricted Web Access / Mature Themes -> 17+); Google Play IARC (unrestricted AI text -> Mature 17+)
findings.evidence: AgentLoop.swift:59-61 returns raw LLM output. An unrestricted GGUF (Mistral/LLaMA) can produce sexual/violent/extremist text on direct prompting. Both stores treat this as a Mature signal regardless of intent.
findings.remediation: File 17+ at submission (questionnaire only). Filing lower and having a reviewer discover the capability is grounds for removal. A lower rating becomes defensible only if an output filter is added later.

findings.item: README markets 'Offline, Autonomous Computer Usage' / ADB device control the store apps do not have
findings.policyRef: App Store Review Guidelines 2.3.1 (Accurate Metadata); Google Play Deceptive Behavior
findings.evidence: README.md:1-5 ('drive Android and Desktop interfaces autonomously'); README.md:35,40-43 (ADB view-hierarchy + adb shell input tap). STORE_SUBMISSION.md:49 instructs the submitter to 'Call out the autonomous device-control / agent feature' - a capability absent from store apps (AgentTool.swift, AgentToolsExtra.swift, AgentScreen.kt: only echo/calculator/units/date).
findings.remediation: (1) Write store-specific copy describing offline chat + pure-math agent. (2) Scope device-control mentions to the desktop Electron product. (3) Fix STORE_SUBMISSION.md:49 so App Review notes do not claim a non-existent feature. Major because the review-notes instruction is live, actionable danger.

compliant.item: 2.5.2 - GGUF files are read-only tensor data (weights), not executable code; like a CoreML .mlpackage
1.2 - single-user offline generator; no sharing/feed/server -> multi-user moderation not in scope
5.1.1 - no unnecessary NSUsageDescription strings; no AVFoundation/CoreLocation/Contacts/Photos usage
5.1.2 - 'Data Not Collected' nutrition label accurate (no analytics/crash/ads SDKs; ConversationStore local-file only)
2.1 - deployment target iOS 16 meets the current new-submission floor (project.yml:8)
Export compliance - only standard OS crypto (TLS + SHA-256); exempt under EAR 742.15(b)
No system entitlements beyond app sandbox; agent tools are local math/date/unit only
Portrait-only orientation acceptable for a chat/agent app (Info.plist:27)
Target API - targetSdk 35 / compileSdk 35 / minSdk 28 exceeds the current Play floor (build.gradle.kts:23,27,29)
Permissions - exactly 5, each justified; no SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW / QUERY_ALL_PACKAGES / contacts / camera / mic / accessibility (manifest:5-11)
Device & Network Abuse - no AccessibilityService/overlay/ADB/screen capture/inter-app control in the source
FGS runtime type-selection correct on API 34+ (ModelDownloadWorker.kt:65-71); gap is the missing manifest <service>, not the runtime code
Content rating - text-only, no sharing/violence/sexual/ads/IAP -> expected Everyone / PEGI 3 (with the unrestricted-LLM caveat under BOTH-M2)
Data collection disclosures - 'Data Not Collected / No Data Shared' accurate (STORE_SUBMISSION.md:38-41,58-62)
SafetyBlocklist on agent tool calls - gates both name and input before execution; Swift/Kotlin parity (AgentLoop.swift:63-68, SafetyBlocklist.swift:12-20, SafetyBlocklist.kt:9-17)
Agent tools are pure on-device logic - no automation/accessibility/screen capture; AgentTool.swift docstring notes iOS sandboxing forbids driving another app
No accessibility/overlay/package-query permissions - Device & Network Abuse clean (manifest:5-11)
FOREGROUND_SERVICE_DATA_SYNC declared and matches actual use (large model download)

remediationOrder: Phase 1 (unblock submission): 1) Host privacy policy + fill URL in App Store Connect & Play Console + in-app row; 2) Android <service> FGS-type fix; 3) Add iOS PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy with E174.1 + 3B52.1

Phase 2 (clear major risks): 4) Report/Flag affordance in chat+agent; 5) File 17+ / Mature 17+; 6) Onboarding disclaimer + output-side keyword filter; 7) Fix STORE_SUBMISSION.md:49 + accurate store copy/review notes; 8) Opt into Apple Standard EULA; 9) Decide on BackgroundModelDownloader

Phase 3 (cleanup/friction): 10) ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false; 11) Android backup_rules.xml excluding models/; 12) Data Safety + store-description network note; 13) iOS 4.2 App Review notes (size range, Wi-Fi, onboarding is first-use)
topActions: Host a privacy policy and fill the URL in App Store Connect + Play Console + an in-app About/Privacy row (unblocks BOTH stores)

Android: add <service android:name="androidx.work.impl.foreground.SystemForegroundService" android:foregroundServiceType="dataSync" tools:node="merge"/> + xmlns:tools (unblocks Play; prevents API 34+ crash)
iOS: add PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy to the app/QuenderinKit target (NSPrivacyTracking=false, empty data arrays, required-reason E174.1 + 3B52.1) (unblocks the upload validator)
Add a Report/Flag affordance in chat + agent UIs and file age rating at 17+/Mature 17+ on both stores (Generative AI + age-rating policy)
Fix STORE_SUBMISSION.md:49 and write store copy/review notes that do NOT claim device control; scope ADB language to the desktop Electron product (accurate-metadata)
